scenes.narration: A deterministic dry run of the product journey QA pipeline.
scenes.subtitle: constructorfabric/gears-rust · Core maintainer
scenes.title: Product Journey QA

scenes.body:
discover_product: planned
follow_tutorial: planned
onboard_project: captured
plan_project_work: cached_validated
fix_bug: cached_validated
file_product_issue: planned
score_and_report: cached_validated
scenes.narration: The run records every expected stage before live or cassette evidence is attached.
scenes.title: 20260705T225534Z-gears-rust-core-maintainer-dwf3-a3-onboard

scenes.body:
Starting surface: terminal-first; prefer TUI/session state before browser surfaces
First question: Will this produce a minimal, reviewable diff that follows the repository's style?
Evidence emphasis: candidate diff, targeted tests, full-suite classification, and trace events for unexpected routing
Escalation trigger: generated churn, hidden broad scope, missing deterministic test proof, or unclear ownership boundary
Finding bias: Prefer reviewability, bisectability, and least-surprise findings over cosmetic notes.
scenes.narration: The persona lens explains how this reviewer should start, what they should question first, and which evidence matters most.
scenes.title: Core maintainer

scenes.body: Project onboarding: onboard_project (session.open, session.inspect, render.tui, visual.observe)
scenes.narration: Each scenario names the story, MCP tools, evidence, and success criteria expected from a real run.
scenes.title: Repeatable tasks

scenes.narration: The execution plan turns each scenario into concrete MCP capture steps and attach commands.
scenes.title: MCP capture steps

scenes.narration: The driver plan gives the product-journey QA agent machine-readable harness, visual surface, and evidence instructions.
scenes.title: Harness and visual surfaces

scenes.narration: The driver contract shows whether each scenario still follows the standard open, observe, act, capture, and journal loop.
scenes.title: Reusable action loop

scenes.narration: Filed product-journey issues and gh-agent fix runs are kept together so reviewers can inspect both the original evidence and the autonomous repair evidence.
scenes.title: Filed issues and autonomous fix runs

scenes.body: project-onboarding: strength 0, weakness 0, issue 0, fix 0, blocked 0
scenes.narration: This matrix shows which scenarios produced strengths, weaknesses, issues, fixes, or blockers.
scenes.title: Findings by scenario

scenes.body: No open observed weakness findings need PRD/design routing.
scenes.narration: Open weakness findings are routed to the PRD/design pipeline instead of being filed as bugfix work.
scenes.title: Weaknesses routed to design

scenes.narration: Each scenario is summarized separately so natural-use gaps remain visible after the bundle-level review passes.
scenes.title: Per-scenario status

scenes.body: project-onboarding: proof 1/5 minimum evidence (captured 1), outcome evidence_captured - The persona can identify the generated project profile, the relevant commands/files, and the next Kitsoki story to launch.
scenes.narration: Quality gates show whether each scenario has the minimum evidence needed before a live or cassette-backed journey is considered complete.
scenes.title: Minimum scenario proof

scenes.body:
needs_evidence: 22/30 checks passed, 3 warnings, 5 failures
fail: driver-journal-coverage - Every non-blocked scenario has a driver journal event.
fail: playback-or-blocker - The review deck has playback media or an explicit blocked-scenario reason for missing playback.
fail: findings-summary - Strengths, weaknesses, issues, or fixes are recorded.
fail: observed-findings - Runs with real proof evidence record at least one observed (non-seeded) finding.
fail: quality-gates - Every scenario satisfies its minimum proof gate or records an explicit blocker.
warn: key-video - At least one key interaction video is attached for Slidey playback.
warn: media-manifest - Captured visual media is listed in the playback manifest.
warn: balanced-findings - Findings include positive evidence and at least one gap or fix.
pass: required-files - All required bundle files exist.
pass: scenario-contract - Scenario and evidence contracts are present.
scenes.narration: The review gate checks whether the bundle has enough evidence and findings to discuss.
scenes.title: needs_evidence

scenes.body: No playback media attached yet. Expected media: product discovery screenshots, onboarding frames, bugfix video, PRD/design captures, feature implementation captures, and product bug filing evidence.
scenes.narration: Slidey scenes carry structured playback media for key visual interactions.
scenes.title: Key interactions

scenes.narration: Captured artifacts are linked back to the scenarios that produced them.
scenes.title: Attached artifacts

scenes.body: Visual MCP frames, Kitsoki traces, oracle results, and video clips will turn this dry run into a reviewable journey deck.
scenes.narration: The next iteration attaches real visual and trace evidence to these scenes.
scenes.title: Evidence to attach
